Babur landed in front of Rāprī and reached the camp at Fatḥpūr at the Bed-time Prayer.
Babur stayed one day at Fatḥpūr on Friday, Jumāda I. 25th.
Babur got to horse on Saturday, Jumāda I. 26th after making ablution at dawn.
Babur went through the Morning Prayer in assembly near Rāprī with Maulānā Muḥammad of Fārāb as leader.
Babur got into a boat below the great crook of Rāprī at sun-rise on Saturday, Jumāda I. 26th.
Babur put together a line-marker of eleven lines to write the mixed hands of the translation on Saturday, Jumāda I. 26th.
Babur was admonished in his heart by the words of a honoured man-of-God on Saturday, Jumāda I. 26th.
Babur had the boats drawn to the bank opposite Jākīn and spent the night in them on Sunday, Jumāda I. 27th.
Babur had the boats moved on from Jākīn before dawn after the Morning Prayer on Sunday, Jumāda I. 27th.
Babur received Pay-master Sl. Muḥammad who brought a servant of Khwāja Kalān named Shamsu'd-dīn Muḥammad while on board.
Babur learned particulars about the affairs of Kābul from the letters and information of Shamsu'd-dīn Muḥammad.
Babur received Mahdī Khwāja while in the boat.
Babur landed in a garden opposite Etāwa at the Mid-day Prayer.
Babur stayed one day at Fatḥpūr on Friday before departing on Saturday.
Babur performed ablution at dawn before traveling on Saturday.
Babur participated in the Morning Prayer in assembly near Rāprī with Maulānā Muḥammad of Fārāb as the leader.
Babur got into a boat below the great crook of Rāprī at sunrise.
Babur created a line-marker of eleven lines to write the mixed hands of a translation.
Babur was admonished by the words of a honoured man-of-God.
Babur received information about the affairs of Kābul from letters brought by Shamsu'd-dīn Muḥammad.
Babur bathed in the Jūn river and fulfilled the duty of prayer at the Mid-day Prayer.
Babur had braves amuse him while sitting in a garden overlooking the river.
Babur delayed for two or three days to collect the army and write letters in response to Shamsu'd-dīn Muḥammad.
Babur marched from Etāwa on Wednesday and traveled 8 kurohs to Mūrī-and-Adūsa.
Babur wrote letters to Humāyūn instructing him to stop raiders and thieves.
Babur summoned Hind-āl.
Babur bestowed Multān on Kāmrān.
Babur had a strong desire to go to Kābul.
Babur believed matters in Hindūstān were coming to settlement.
Babur was emotionally affected by eating a melon, being moved to tears.
Babur had repented and vowed to sin no more.
Babur summoned his elder sister Khān-zāda and his wives to Hindūstān.
Babur ordered the repair of the fort in Kābul.
Babur ordered the provision of stores in Kābul.
Babur ordered money to be spent for building the Congregational Mosque in Kābul.
Babur ordered repairs of the Kārwan-sarā and Hot-baths in Kābul.
Babur ordered the completion of an unfinished building made of burnt-brick in the citadel.
Babur ordered the construction of the Khẉurd-Kābul dam.
Babur ordered the repair of the Ghaznī dam.
Babur ordered the Avenue-garden to be improved with diverted water.
Babur had water brought from Tūtūm-dara to create a reservoir and plant young trees.
Babur named a place Belvedere because it faces the ford and gives a first-rate view.
Babur named Sayyid Qāsim to reinforce Khwāja Kalān.
Babur ordered his elder sister Khān-zāda Begīm and his wives to be escorted out of Kābul to Nīl-āb.
Babur had experienced great confusion in his mind regarding renouncement of wine.
Babur had vowed to abstain from wine and was repentant.
Babur had longed for a wine-party infinitely for two years past.
Babur's craving for wine sometimes brought him to the verge of tears.
Babur believed the trouble of wine-craving had passed from his mind by the blessing of versifying the translation.
Babur advised Khwāja Kalān to renounce wine.
Babur entrusted letters to Shamsu'd-dīn Muḥammad on Friday night.
Babur traveled 8 kurohs and dismounted at Jumandnā on Friday.
Babur issued orders to the begs of the border to put an end to raiding and thieving.
Babur sent a letter accepting excuse for the belated arrival of Ḥasan Chalabī.
Babur traveled 8 kurohs on Saturday and dismounted in the Kakūra and Chachāwalī parganas of Kālpī.
Babur traveled 9 kurohs on Sunday and dismounted in Dīrapūr.
Babur shaved his head at Dīrapūr, which he had not done for two months.
Babur bathed in the Sīngar-water at Dīrapūr.
Babur traveled 14 kurohs on Monday and dismounted in Chaparkada.
Babur learned that Māhīm was on the road and had summoned escort.
Babur traveled 7 kurohs on Wednesday and dismounted in the Ādampūr pargana.
Babur mounted before dawn and traveled alone to the Jūn river.
Babur had awnings set up on an island near the camp and ate ma'jūn.
Babur set Ṣādiq to wrestle with Kalāl.
Babur rewarded Ṣādiq with 10,000 tankas, a saddled horse, a head-to-foot, and a jacket with buttons for winning the wrestling match.
Babur gave Kalāl 3000 tankas despite his loss in the wrestling match.
Babur ordered carts and mortar to be landed from boats.
Babur spent 3 or 4 days having the road made ready and ground levelled.
Babur traveled by litter on Monday the 12th of the month.
Babur traveled 12 kurohs from Kūrarah and dismounted in Kūrīa.
Babur traveled 8 kurohs from Kūrīa and dismounted in Fatḥpūr-Aswa.
Babur traveled 8 kurohs from Fatḥpūr and dismounted at Sarāī Munda.
Sl. Jalālu'd-dīn came with his two young sons to wait on Babur at Bedtime Prayer on Friday.
Babur traveled 8 kurohs on Saturday and dismounted at Dugdugī on the bank of the Gang.
Muḥammad Sl. M., Nī-khūb Sl., and Tardīka came to Dugdugī on Sunday.
Askarī waited on Babur on Monday.
Babur ordered Askarī and his forces to march along the other bank of the river opposite the army.
Babur learned that Sl. Maḥmūd had collected 10,000 Afghāns.
Babur learned that Shaikh Bāyazīd and Bīban were detached towards Sarwār.
Babur learned that Sher Khān Sūr, whom he had favored with the gift of several parganas, had joined the Afghāns.
Babur learned that Sl. Jalālu'd-dīn's man in Benares had fled.
Babur marched from Dugdugī on Tuesday and traveled 6 kurohs to Kusār.
Babur went by boat from Kusār.
Babur stayed at Kusār for 3 or 4 days because of hospitality offered by Sl. Jalālu'd-dīn.
Babur dismounted at Sl. Jalālu'd-dīn's house inside Karrah-fort on Friday.
Sl. Jalālu'd-dīn served Babur cooked meat and other viands.
Babur dressed Sl. Jalālu'd-dīn and his sons in unlined coats and short tunics.
Babur gave Sl. Jalālu'd-dīn's elder son the style Sl. Maḥmūd at his request.
Babur rode about one kuroh from Karrah and dismounted on the bank of Gang.
Babur gave leave to Shahrak Beg who had come from Māhīm.
Babur sent a copy of his records to Khwāja Kalān by Shahrak Beg.
Babur traveled by boat on Saturday and halted at Koh after 4 kurohs.
Babur ate ma'jūn inside a boat at Koh.
Babur invited Khwāja Abdu'sh-shahīd and Mullā Maḥmūd to join him.
Babur crossed the river and set wrestlers to wrestle on the other side.
Babur told Dost-i-yāsīn-khair not to grapple with Champion Ṣādiq but with others.
Babur learned that the army of Sl. Maḥmūd had broken up.
Babur learned that rebels had laid siege to Chunār but rose in disorderly fashion.
Babur learned that Afghāns who had crossed the river for Benares turned back in disorder.
Babur learned that two Afghan boats had sunk and a body of their men had drowned.
Babur marched at Sunday's dawn and traveled 6 kurohs to Sīr-auliya.
Babur traveled by boat directly to Sīr-auliya.
Aīsan-tīmūr Sl. and Tūkhta-būghā Sl. were waiting to see Babur halfway.
Babur invited Aīsan-tīmūr Sl. and Tūkhta-būghā Sl. into his boat.
Babur ate ma'jūn on the day at Sīr-auliya despite having done so the previous day.
Babur visited an island opposite the camp by boat on Tuesday.
Babur's horse got to where the riverbank was fissured and began to give way.
Babur leapt off his horse and flung himself on the bank to avoid falling into the river.
Babur swam the Gang-river, counting every stroke.
Babur crossed the Gang-river with 33 strokes and swam back without resting.
Babur had swum other rivers but Gang had remained to do.
Babur ate ma'jun on a windy day despite having eaten it the previous day.
Babur visited an island by boat opposite his camp.
Babur's horse nearly fell into a fissured ravine while he rode carelessly along a river.
Babur swam the Ganges river and counted every stroke, crossing with 33 strokes.
Babur had previously swum other rivers before swimming the Ganges.
Babur swam back across the Ganges without resting after his first crossing.
The army used 420 boats to cross the Jūn river.
Babur ordered wrestlers to wrestle during the march.
Babur waited two days to examine a ford and repair the road across the Tūs river.
Babur traveled by boat while his army crossed rivers.
Tāj Khān Sārang-khānī came from Chunār to meet Babur with his two young sons.
Babur's family and train were reported to be on their way from Kābul.
Babur developed painful boils on his body during the march from Pīāg.
An Ottoman Turk treated Babur's boils with a remedy involving boiled pepper steam.
Babur held his sores in steam from boiled pepper for 2 sidereal hours.
Babur went hunting for tiger and rhinoceros but neither appeared.
A wild buffalo appeared at the end of a hunting line instead of tiger or rhinoceros.
Babur decided to march continuously, fast and far after hearing news of Maḥmūd Khān Lūdi near the Son-water.
Babur sent Ṭāhir to Āgra with money-drafts for customary gifts.
Babur examined the ground where he had previously started for Jūnpūr.
Babur ordered Mughūl Beg to measure all marches from Chunār on the direct road.
Babur ordered Luṯfī Beg to measure the river's bank when Babur traveled by boat.
Maḥmūd Khān Nuḥānī waited on Babur.
Babur received dutiful letters from several Afghan amīrs including Sher Khān Sūr and `Alāūl Khān Sūr.
Babur's family met `Abdu'l-`azīz at Nīl-āb on the 9th of the latter Jumāda.
Babur landed opposite Chausā to look at the ground where a solar eclipse had occurred a year prior.
A solar eclipse and fast had occurred at Chausā a year before.
Babur ate ma'jun after Muḥammad-i-zamān Mīrzā suggested it.
Hindus are extremely scrupulous about the Karmā-nā['s]ā-river and do not cross it.
Hindus believe that if the Karmā-nā['s]ā-river water touches a person, the merit of his works is destroyed.
Muḥsin the cup-bearer challenged to grapple with four or five men.
Shādmān threw Muḥsin, causing him shame and vexation.
Babur used the pepper remedy again and it blistered his body, causing much pain.
Babur waited a day for a road to be managed across a swampy rivulet.
Between 40 and 50 landing-steps had been made on the bank at Baksara a year prior.
The river had destroyed all but the upper two landing-steps at Baksara.
Babur had eaten opium a year ago during an excursion.
Babur sent out not under 200 effective braves led by Karīm-bīrdī and Ḥaidar's son Muḥammad `Alī.
Babur commanded the Bengal envoy to set forth three articles.
Yūnas-i-`alī brought back a weak answer regarding Muḥammad-i-zamān Mīrzā's objections to Bihār.
The Farmūlī Shaikh-zādas of Bihār reported that the enemy had abandoned Bihār.
Babur joined 2000 men of Turk and Hind amīrs to Tardī-muḥammad and gave him leave to go to Bihār.
Khwāja Murshid `Irāqī was made Dīwān of Bihār.
Muḥammad-i-zamān Mīrzā asked for reinforcement for Bihār.
Babur rode to visit Bhūjpūr and Bihiya.
Muḥammad `Alī and others sent for news beat a body of pagans.
Maḥmūd Lūdī had perhaps 2000 men when encountered by Babur's scouts.
Maḥmūd Lūdī killed two of his own elephants and marched off upon hearing of Babur's reconnaissance.
Babur's men unhorsed some of Maḥmūd Lūdī's men and cut off one head.
Muḥammad-i-zamān Mīrzā crossed his army over the Son river.
Babur spent 2 or 3 days on a ground to complete Muḥammad-i-zamān Mīrzā's work.
Babur presented Muḥammad-i-zamān Mīrzā with a royal head-to-foot, sword, belt, horse, and umbrella.
Muḥammad-i-zamān Mīrzā was made to kneel for the Bihār country.
One krūr and 25 laks of Bihār revenues were reserved for the Royal Treasury.
Babur ordered boats to be fastened together abreast in line.
A crocodile appeared in the river, causing a terrified fish to leap into a boat.
A large boat formerly called the Bāburī was renamed Asāīsh (Repose).
The Bāburī boat had been built in Āgra before the Holy-battle with Sangā.
Arāīsh Khān built and presented a boat to Babur before the army got to horse.
Babur had a platform set up on a boat presented by Arāīsh Khān.
Jalālu'd-dīn Sharqī presented a good-sized boat to Babur, which was named the Gunjāīsh (Capacious).
Babur ordered a second platform set up on top of the existing one in the Gunjāīsh boat.
A little skiff with a chaukandī was named Farmāīsh (Commissioned).
Muḥammad-i-zamān Mīrzā came to take leave of Babur after completing preparations for Bihār.
Two spies reported that Bengalīs under Makhdūm-i-`ālam were posted in 24 places on the Gandak.
Bengalīs had hindered Afghāns from carrying out their intention to get their families across the river.
Babur detained Muḥammad-i-zamān Mīrzā upon hearing news of probable fighting.
Babur sent Shāh Iskandar to Bihār with 3 or 400 men.
Dūdū and her son Jalāl Khān came from the Bengalīs.
Dūdū and Jalāl Khān did straight fighting to get away from the Bengalīs.
Dūdū and Jalāl Khān crossed the river and reached Bihār.
Babur commanded the Bengal envoy Ismā`īl Mītā to write that an answer to three articles was long in coming.
Tardī-muḥammad's scouts reached Bihār and the Shiqdār went off by a gate on the other side.
Babur marched on and dismounted in the pargana of Ārī (Ārrah).
The Kharīd army had 100-150 boats and was near the meeting of Sarū and Gang.
A sort of peace existed between Babur and the Bengalī Naṣrat Shāh.
Peace was Babur's first endeavour whenever such work was toward.
Babur associated Mullā Maẕhab with the Bengal envoy Ismā`īl Mītā.
Babur decided to let the Bengal envoy go.
Babur told the Bengal envoy he would pursue his foe but do no hurt to any dependency of Bengal.
Babur instructed the Bengal envoy to tell the Kharīd army to rise off his road and go back to Kharīd.
Babur offered to join a few Turks with the Kharīd army to reassure and escort them.
Babur warned that if the Kharīd army did not quit the ferry-head, any ill that befell them would be their own act.
The Bengal envoy was given the usual dress of honour and gifts.
Shaikh Jamālī was sent with royal letters of encouragement to Dūdū and Jalāl Khān.
Babur instructed the Bengal envoy that his dependencies would not be harmed during military operations.
Babur sent Shaikh Jamālī with royal letters of encouragement to Dūdū and her son Jalāl Khān.
Babur sent Khalīfa to scout crossing points on the Ganges river.
Babur sent Khalīfa again to examine the ground between the Ganges and Ghogrā rivers.
Babur rode southward to visit lotus sheets near Ārī.
Babur received fresh-set lotus-seeds from Shaikh Gūran during his excursion.
Babur crossed the Son river and visited the tomb of Shaikh Yaḥyā.
Babur performed ablution and prayed the Mid-day Prayer before time at the Son-bank.
Babur ordered a measurement of the distance traveled, which amounted to 23,100 horse steps.
Babur calculated his total excursion distance as approximately 30 kurohs (60 miles).
Babur expressed blame and displeasure to Sl. Junaid Barlās for his delay.
Babur did not see Sl. Junaid Barlās due to his displeasure.
Babur summoned Turk and Hind amīrs for consultation about crossing the Ganges.
Babur ordered Ūstād `Alī-qulī to position artillery between the Sarū and Ganges rivers.
Babur ordered Muṣṯafa to position matchlockmen on the Bihār side of the Ganges.
Babur ordered shelters to be built for culverin-firers.
Babur ordered `Askarī and the sulṯāns to cross at the Haldī-passage.
Babur ordered Zard-rūī to scout a crossing-place 8 kurohs higher up.
Babur decided to cross the Ganges at the Haldī-passage by boat.
Babur ordered Ūstād `Alī-qulī and Muṣṯafa to engage the enemy with artillery and firearms.
Babur formed the force north of the Ganges into four divisions under `Askarī's command.
Babur crossed the Ganges by boat at the 1st watch (6 a.m.).
Babur went to observe Ūstād `Alī-qulī's firing of culverin and firingī.
Babur noted that Ūstād `Alī-qulī hit and sank two boats with firingī-stones.
Babur had the large mortar taken to the fighting-ground.
Babur appointed Mullā Ghulām to superintend the mortar's positioning.
Babur went to an island facing the camp and ate ma`jūn.
Babur slept in a boat while under the influence of ma`jūn.
Babur was nearly attacked by an intruder during the night.
Babur expressed faith that God protected him from harm.
Babur posted men to their work from the boat Gunjāīsh.
Babur sent Aūghān-bīrdī Mughūl with 1,000 men to cross the Sarū river higher up.
Babur observed Ūstād `Alī-qulī fire a large stone and several small firingī stones.
Babur noted that Bengalīs fire artillery at random rather than at particular targets.
Babur ordered boats to be drawn up-stream along the enemy's front.
Babur ordered Aīsān-tīmūr Sl. and Tūkhta-būghā Sl. to keep watch over boats.
Babur quickly mounted his men and rode out to the boats drawn up in the night.
Babur sent a galloper with orders for Muḥammad Sl. M. to cross and join `Askarī.
Babur ordered Aīsān-tīmūr Sl. and Tūkhta-būghā Sl. to cross the river.
Babur praised Aīsān-tīmūr Sl. for crossing first and charging the enemy foot-soldiers.
Babur ordered the Sulṯāns to gather those who crossed and flank the opposing army.
Babur sent a man to gallop off with tactical orders to the Sulṯāns.
Babur sent for his own boats which were behind.
Babur went over in the Farmāīsh to visit the Bengalīs' encamping-grounds.
Babur asked about crossing-places higher up on the Sarū.
Babur ordered the army-folk to cross at a higher place on the Sarū.
Babur bestowed Yakka Khwāja's retainers and lands on his younger brother Qāsim Khwāja after Yakka Khwāja drowned.
